title: How to Deploy a Website on Vercel

steps.title: Open Vercel New Project Page
steps.description: Navigate to vercel.com/new in your browser. This is the starting point for deploying any website on Vercel. You will see the main page with a Git repository URL input field, options to Import a Git Repository or Clone a Template, and login buttons for GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket.

steps.title: Explore Import and Template Options
steps.description: Scroll down to see the two main deployment paths. On the left, the Import Git Repository section lets you connect your GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket account. On the right, the Clone Template section shows popular starter templates like Next.js Boilerplate, Chatbot, Vite + React, and Express.js on Vercel.

steps.title: View Create Empty Project Option
steps.description: At the bottom of the page, you will find the Create Empty Project option. This allows you to skip Git setup entirely and instantly access Vercel products like Web Analytics and Speed Insights. This is useful if you want to deploy manually using the Vercel CLI instead of connecting a repository.

steps.title: Browse the Template Gallery
steps.description: Click Browse All to open the full template gallery. Here you will see the Find your Template page with a search bar and filter options. Templates are organized by use case categories like AI, Starter, Ecommerce, SaaS, Blog, Portfolio, CMS, and Backend, making it easy to find the right starting point.

steps.title: Filter Templates by Use Case
steps.description: Use the Filter Templates sidebar on the left to narrow down your choices. Check categories like AI, Starter, Ecommerce, or Blog to find templates that match your project needs. The main area displays template cards with preview images, names, and short descriptions so you can quickly evaluate each option.

steps.title: Discover Framework-Specific Templates
steps.description: Continue scrolling to find more specialized templates organized by framework and use case. You will see options like Nextra Docs Starter Kit, Express on Bun, Hono on Bun, Hume AI Voice, Next.js Commerce, and many more. The left sidebar also shows advanced categories like Edge Functions, Edge Middleware, and Cron.

steps.title: Explore Advanced Template Categories
steps.description: Scroll further to discover templates for platforms, portfolios, enterprise blogs, and multi-tenant applications. The sidebar reveals even more filter categories including Realtime Apps, Documentation, Virtual Events, Monorepos, Web3, Vercel Firewall, Microfrontends, and Authentication.

steps.title: Select a Template and Deploy
steps.description: Choose any template that fits your project by clicking on its card. Vercel will then guide you through naming your project, configuring build settings, and adding environment variables. Once configured, click the Deploy button and Vercel will build and deploy your website to a global CDN with a unique URL in minutes.
